title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
slides: OpenAI built its own chip. But why now?
Your API costs could drop by half overnight.
Test a single prompt on both models today.
thread_text:
1. Why build a chip now?

OpenAI just announced its own chip. But they skipped the latest lithography entirely.

2. Inference over training

This chip targets inference, not training. That means faster responses and lower cost per query.

3. API costs cut in half

Your API costs could drop by half overnight. The savings come from the inference-first design.

4. Not cutting edge

It skips cutting-edge lithography. Older nodes are cheaper and good enough for inference workloads.

5. Test it yourself today

Take a prompt on ChatGPT and run it through both models. Does the new chip actually save you money?

instagram.caption:
Your API bill could be cut in half overnight.

OpenAI built its own chip called Jalapeño, but skipped cutting-edge lithography. It’s designed purely for inference—not training—so responses get faster and cheaper.

For anyone running prompts through the API, your per-query costs drop immediately. No waiting for model changes.

Take one prompt you use today and run it through both models—does your wallet actually feel the difference? 🧠💸⚡

carousel_slides.description: OpenAI's Jalapeño chip uses older nodes to cut costs, not performance. Inference workloads don't need bleeding-edge tech.
Jalapeño targets inference, not training. That means faster responses and lower cost per query for your API calls.
Your API costs could drop by 50% overnight. The savings come from the inference-first design and cheaper nodes.
Jalapeño skips cutting-edge lithography. Older nodes are cheaper and good enough for inference workloads.
Run the same prompt on ChatGPT and Jalapeño. See if the new chip actually saves you money today.
